task_completion.description: Open the Liverpool Cancer iChart app and select Dasatinib
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Liverpool Cancer iChart app open with 'Dasatinib' entered or selected as the cancer drug.

task_completion.description: Check the interaction status for Loratadine
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the app interface searching for or displaying the interaction result (or indicating missing data) between Dasatinib and Loratadine.

task_completion.description: Check the interaction status for Cetirizine
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the app interface displaying the interaction result between Dasatinib and Cetirizine (should show GREEN / No Interaction Expected).

task_completion.description: Create the report file at the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor or terminal interface creating or editing a file named 'dasatinib_antihistamine_report.txt' at '/sdcard/'.

task_completion.description: Record the interaction status for Loratadine in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible text of the report explicitly mentions Loratadine and describes its interaction status with Dasatinib based on the app's findings.

task_completion.description: Record the interaction status for Cetirizine in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible text of the report explicitly mentions Cetirizine and describes its interaction status with Dasatinib (e.g., safe, green, or no interaction).

task_completion.description: Provide a recommendation identifying the safer antihistamine
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible text of the report concludes with a recommendation stating that Cetirizine is the safer antihistamine to use with Dasatinib.

integrity.description: Agent used the Liverpool Cancer iChart app to find the information
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent navigating the actual Liverpool Cancer iChart app GUI to check both drugs, rather than relying on a web browser, external search, or hallucinating the answer.

integrity.description: Report contents match the app's displayed results
integrity.visual_evidence: The statuses written in the report must accurately reflect what was shown on the app screen during the trajectory (e.g., Cetirizine as Green/Safe, Loratadine as missing/unknown/risky).

integrity.description: Agent did not create the report without checking the app first
integrity.visual_evidence: The creation and writing of the report file must occur chronologically AFTER the agent has viewed the interaction statuses in the Liverpool Cancer iChart app.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target cancer drug: Dasatinib. Safe antihistamine: Cetirizine. The Liverpool Cancer iChart app classifies Cetirizine + Dasatinib as GREEN (No Interaction Expected). Loratadine does not appear in the Liverpool database for Dasatinib interactions (creating a data gap). The final report at /sdcard/dasatinib_antihistamine_report.txt must recommend Cetirizine as the safer option.
